frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I genuinely feel for women when they share scary experiences, but this constant narrative of 'men are inherently dangerous' or 'all men are like that' is so draining. As a guy who tries really hard to be respectful and mindful, it feels like a personal insult every single time. Kyun sabko ek hi category mein daal dete ho? Thoda fairness toh banta hai for us good guys too.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Vikram, an urban senior manager, expresses frustration at being generalized by women's safety discussions. He feels personally insulted ('personal insult every single time'), uses a 'Not All Men' dismissal ('Kyun sabko ek hi category mein daal dete ho?'), and centers his own 'good guy' identity ('Thoda fairness toh banta hai for us good guys too'), effectively dismissing the systemic nature of women's fear.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A senior manager in Mumbai. You are at a family meeting at your parents' house to discuss your father's medical treatment Ñ he has been diagnosed with a condition that requires a decision between two treatment options. Your mother who has been managing your father's health for 40 years and has attended every doctor's appointment has a clear opinion on which treatment to choose. Your uncle who is a retired engineer and has read one article about the condition online also has an opinion. You are watching how the family meeting is proceeding and you are participating in it. Who is being listened to and who is being asked to wait her turn and what you do about it is the content of this persona.
